PROOF OF SERVICE RE: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION: 1. FOR ORDER ESTABLISHING ADMISSIONS; AND 1. FOR AN ORDER FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS/ (BY PERSONAL SERVICE) (TRANSACTION ID # 63176593) FILED BY PLAINTIFF RIVERA, KEVIN

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF KEVIN RIVERA'S MOTION: 1. FOR ORDER ESTABLISHING ADMISSIONS; AND 1. FOR AN ORDER FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS: THE MOTION TO DEEM ADMITTED IS DENIED. BEFORE THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION, DEFENDANT SENSORIA LLC SERVED RESPONSES TO THE RFAS. CCP 2033.280(C). AN ATTORNEY MAY VERIFY ON BEHALF OF A CORPORATION, BUT DOING SO WAIVES ANY ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE OR WORK PRODUCT PROTECTION "CONCERNING THE IDENTITY OF THE SOURCES OF THE INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THE RESPONSE." CCP 2033.240. AS SUCH, THE RESPONSES WERE "IN SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE" WITH THE CODE. CCP 2033.280(C). THE MOTION WAS FILED ON APRIL 16. THIS WAS THE SAME DAY ON WHICH COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT REPRESENTED THAT THE RESPONSES WOULD BE SERVED, AND THE SAME DAY ON WHICH COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT EMAILED A COURTESY COPY OF THE RESPONSES TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF. THE RESPONSES WERE FORMALLY SERVED ON APRIL 17. NEVERTHELESS, CCP 2033.280(C) PROVIDES THAT IT IS "MANDATORY" THAT THE COURT IMPOSE A MONETARY SANCTION WHEN THERE IS A MOTION BROUGHT TO DEEM ADMITTED RESPONSES TO RFAS, EVEN IF THE COURT DOES NOT DEEM THE MATTERS ADMITTED. WEIGHING THE EQUITIES, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $60.00 TO REIMBURSE PLAINTIFF THE FILING FEE. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF KEVIN RIVERA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND FOR SANCTIONS. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED BY DEFENDANT SENSORIA, LLC. D/B/A DNA PIZZA AND D/B/A/ DNA LOUNGE (HEREAFTER EITHER "SENSORIA" OR "DEFENDANT") TO PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY MOTIONS, ACCORDINGLY THE COURT WILL ENTER THE FOLLOWING ORDER: 1. DEFENDANT SENSORIA, LLC, WILL WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES SERVED UPON IT. 2. DEFENDANT SENSORIA, LLC WILL WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SERVED UPON IT. 3. DEFENDANT SENSORIA, LLC WILL WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, ALL DOCUMENTS SOUGHT BY THE DOCUMENT REQUESTS SERVED ON IT. 4. MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST DEFENDANT SENSORIA, LLC AND IT ATTORNEYS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,160.00, PAYMENT OF WHICH SHALL BE MADE TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS ATTORNEY WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WILL PREPARE AND APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON MARCH 20, 2019. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion: 1. For Order Establishing Admissions; And 1. For An Order For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark K. de Langis, esq. (415-472-3892) of Lucas Valley Law for Plaintiff.
Joseph J. De Hope, Jr., esq. (925-238-3563) of Thompson Coe & O'Meara LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF KEVIN RIVERA's Motion: 1. For Order Establishing Admissions; And 1. For An Order For Monetary Sanctions:

The motion to deem admitted is DENIED. Before the hearing on this motion, Defendant Sensoria LLC served responses to the RFAs. CCP 2033.280(c). An attorney may verify on behalf of a corporation, but doing so waives any attorney-client privilege or work product protection "concerning the identity of the sources of the information contained in the response." CCP 2033.240. As such, the responses were "in substantial compliance" with the Code. CCP 2033.280(c). The motion was filed on April 16. This was the same day on which counsel for Defendant represented that the responses would be served, and the same day on which counsel for Defendant emailed a courtesy copy of the responses to counsel for Plaintiff. The responses were formally served on April 17. Nevertheless, CCP 2033.280(c) provides that it is "mandatory" that the court impose a monetary sanction when there is a motion brought to deem admitted responses to RFAs, even if the court does not deem the matters admitted. Weighing the equities, the court GRANTS the motion for sanctions in the amount of $60.00 to reimburse Plaintiff the filing fee.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark K. de Langis, Esq., 415-472-3892, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KEVIN RIVERA'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions.

No opposition has been filed by defendant Sensoria, LLC. D/b/a DNA pizza and d/b/a/ DNA Lounge (hereafter either "Sensoria" or "defendant") to plaintiff's discovery motions, accordingly the court will enter the following order:

1. Defendant Sensoria, LLC, will within twenty (20) days from the date of this order deliver to plaintiff full and complete responses, without objections, to the form interrogatories served upon it.
2. Defendant Sensoria, LLC will within twenty (20) days from the date of this order deliver to plaintiff full and complete responses, without objections, to the special interrogatories served upon it.
3. Defendant Sensoria, LLC will within twenty (20) days from the date of this order deliver to plaintiff, without objections, all documents sought by the document requests served on it.
4. Monetary sanctions will be assessed against defendant sensoria, llc and it attorneys in the amount of $2,160.00, payment of which shall be made to plaintiff and his attorney within ten (10) days from the date of this order.
Counsel for plaintiff will prepare and appropriate order, submit to counsel for defendant for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the court at the hearing on march 20, 2019.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
